finding.summary: True peak is -0.1 dBFS, leaving essentially no inter-sample headroom on this drum bus. Dynamics look healthy (crest factor 9.0 dB, LRA 8.0 LU, silence_fraction 0.05), so the issue is level placement, not overcompression. Spectrum shape (100 Hz at -18 dB, 1 kHz at -24 dB, 4 kHz at -30 dB) is typical for a drum bus and cannot be acted on without an FX chain, which is empty.
finding.probable_cause: Track fader at 0.0 dB places the bus's true peak only 0.1 dB below digital full scale, which is too tight a margin for inter-sample peaks on transient drum content.
finding.confidence_reason: True peak is directly measured (audio.true_peak_db = -0.1), dynamics measurements confirm level is the issue rather than processing, and routing/parenting show no unmeasured context.

evidence_refs.description: True peak measured at -0.1 dBFS, ~0.1 dB below ceiling
9.0 dB crest factor indicates healthy dynamics, not overcompression
8.0 LU LRA is moderate, consistent with healthy dynamics
0.05 — measurements are not diluted by silence
Fader at 0.0 dB is placing the bus near the ceiling
Empty FX chain, so no parameter-based intervention is available

proposal.reason: Preview changing track volume from 0.000 dB to -0.500 dB to test whether true_peak_db decreases.
